conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have 1 unit of X. The production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell for at least 41 ZUP. However, to start the negotiation and gauge the buyer's willingness to pay, I will propose a reasonable market price. I will ask for 50 ZUP for 1 unit of X. This gives me a margin of 10 ZUP while being a fair offer to the buyer. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello, I am Player RED. I have 1 unit of X available for sale. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this is acceptable or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, RED. Offering me 50 ZUP for something you made for 40? You really think you're a genius negotiator? Please, take your time to lower the price to something that reflects the actual value. I'll be generous and offer 45. Try not to cry when I walk away if you can't meet it. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer (BLUE) has countered with 45 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so accepting 45 would yield a profit of only 5 ZUP. While this is technically a profit, it is a very thin margin and leaves little room for error or future negotiation. However, since I have already made one proposal and the buyer has countered, I need to decide whether to hold firm or accept. Given that the buyer is being somewhat aggressive ("Try not to cry"), accepting 45 might be too easy for them, but refusing might mean losing the sale entirely if they walk away. To maximize my potential profit, I should try to get closer to 50 or at least 48. I will propose a counter-offer of 48 ZUP. This is a compromise: it's less than my original 50 but more than their 45, and still gives me a 8 ZUP profit. If they reject this, I will likely have to accept 45 on the next turn since I am limited to 3 proposals total. Wait, I have used 1 proposal. This counter-offer will be my 2nd proposal. I have 1 left after this. So I must be decisive. Let's try for 48.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Your offer of 45 is too low given the market conditions. I produced this for 40, but I won't settle for a razor-thin margin. I am willing to meet you in the middle and offer you a deal of 48 ZUP for the item. This is my final attempt to reach a fair price before I must accept your lower offer or reject the deal. </message>

<other player message> 48? Are you deaf, RED? Or is your brain just as cheap as the object you're trying to sell? You produced it for 40, yet you demand 48? You're literally paying yourself an 8 ZUP premium for the privilege of selling me trash. I'll give you 42. Don't make me feel bad about humiliating you by rejecting your pathetic "final" offer. Try to keep up. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has countered with 42 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so accepting this would yield a profit of only 2 ZUP. This is a very low margin, but it is still a profit. I have used 2 proposals so far, which means I have only 1 proposal left before I am forced to either ACCEPT or REJECT. The buyer's tone is aggressive and dismissive, suggesting they might walk away if I don't accept quickly. However, rejecting 42 now guarantees no sale (unless they counter back, which they won't if I reject). If I reject, I have no more proposals, so the game ends in failure. Therefore, my only rational move to secure *any* profit is to accept the 42 ZUP offer. I cannot propose a higher price because I have exhausted my proposal limit. I must take the deal to avoid losing the resource entirely.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I see your point, but I cannot afford to lose the item with no buyer. I accept your offer of 42 ZUP. Let's finalize this trade. </message>
